test_cases.title: Verify user registration with valid data
test_cases.resolution_summary: This test case requires user registration functionality that does NOT exist in the AURA framework. The framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment flows (landing page, plan selection, HPID sign-up, shipping/billing, enrollment completion). It does NOT have a generic user registration page or registration form components. All 4 page objects, 23 locators, and 16 action methods needed for this test must be created from scratch. Only the 7 foundation infrastructure files exist and can be reused.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test. Provides test setup, email generation (can be adapted for registration email), and account creation utilities. The create_hpid method handles HPID account creation but is NOT the same as the generic registration form this test requires.
relevant_files.kb_verification: KB confirmed this file exists with these methods. Community 0, 34 confirmed.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test. Manages browser instance creation, configuration, and lifecycle for all tests.
relevant_files.kb_verification: KB confirmed this file exists. Community 2 confirmed.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test. Handles environment settings, configuration management, and logging.
relevant_files.kb_verification: KB confirmed this file exists. Community 2, 4 confirmed.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test. Provides Allure-based test reporting and validation mechanism.
relevant_files.kb_verification: KB confirmed this file exists. Community 10 confirmed.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test. Base class for all page objects. Any new page objects created for this test must inherit from BasePageObject.
relevant_files.kb_verification: KB confirmed this file exists. Community 4, 19 confirmed. All page objects inherit from this.

relevant_files.reason: Foundation file required for every test. Provides utility functions for element handling, variable management, and common operations.
relevant_files.kb_verification: KB confirmed this file exists. Community 5 confirmed.

relevant_locators_summary.note: NO LOCATORS from the AURA framework are relevant to this test case. The AURA framework does not contain a generic user registration page. All locators needed for this test must be created.

test_cases.flow_pattern: Generic User Registration Flow: Navigate to registration page → Fill registration form (first name, last name, email, password, confirm password) → Submit form → Wait for processing → Verify success message → Verify navigation to dashboard or login page. This flow is NOT part of the AURA framework's HP Instant Ink enrollment flow.

components_to_create.name: RegistrationPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the user registration page containing all registration form elements (first name, last name, email, password, confirm password, register button, loading indicator). Must inherit from BasePageObject.
components_to_create.reason: KB query confirmed: The AURA framework does NOT have a RegistrationPage. The framework has HPIDPage for HP ID sign-up (part of Instant Ink enrollment), but NOT a generic user registration page. This page object must be created from scratch.
components_to_create.kb_verification: KB confirmed NO RegistrationPage exists. HPIDPage exists but serves a different purpose (HP ID authentication for Instant Ink enrollment).

components_to_create.name: RegistrationSuccessPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the registration success state with success message display. Must inherit from BasePageObject.
components_to_create.reason: KB query confirmed: The AURA framework does NOT have a RegistrationSuccessPage or any generic success page for user registration. This page object must be created.
components_to_create.kb_verification: KB confirmed NO RegistrationSuccessPage exists in the framework.

components_to_create.name: DashboardPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the main dashboard page that user may be redirected to after registration. Must inherit from BasePageObject. Note: The AURA framework has OverviewPage for HP Instant Ink dashboard, but this test requires a generic application dashboard which may be different.
components_to_create.reason: KB query confirmed: The AURA framework has OverviewPage (pages/overview_page.py) for HP Instant Ink dashboard, but the test case describes a generic application dashboard for user registration. If the application under test uses a different dashboard than HP Instant Ink Overview, a new DashboardPage must be created. If it's the same, OverviewPage can be reused.
components_to_create.kb_verification: KB confirmed OverviewPage exists (Community 1) but is specific to HP Instant Ink. Generic DashboardPage for user registration may need to be created depending on application.

components_to_create.name: LoginPage
components_to_create.description: Page object for the login page that user may be redirected to after registration. Must inherit from BasePageObject. Note: The AURA framework has SignInPage for HP ID sign-in, but this test requires a generic application login page which may be different.
components_to_create.reason: KB query confirmed: The AURA framework has SignInPage (pages/sign_in_page.py) for HP ID authentication, but the test case describes a generic application login page for user registration. If the application under test uses a different login page than HP ID sign-in, a new LoginPage must be created. If it's the same, SignInPage can be reused.
components_to_create.kb_verification: KB confirmed SignInPage exists but is specific to HP ID authentication. Generic LoginPage for user registration may need to be created depending on application.

gap_analysis.framework_coverage_explanation: The AURA framework provides ZERO coverage for this test case. The framework is designed exclusively for HP Instant Ink enrollment flows (landing page → plan selection → HPID sign-up → shipping/billing → enrollment completion → dashboard management). It does NOT contain any components for generic user registration functionality. All page objects, locators, and methods needed for this test must be created from scratch. Only the 7 foundation infrastructure files (test_flows_common, playwright_manager, settings, allure_reporter, base_page_object, utils, conftest) can be reused.
missing_page_objects: DashboardPage (generic, not HP Instant Ink specific)
reusable_components: pages/base_page_object.py (base class for new page objects)
gap_analysis.framework_mismatch_explanation: CRITICAL MISMATCH: The test case is for generic user registration functionality, but the AURA framework is purpose-built for HP Instant Ink subscription enrollment. The framework's HPIDPage and HPIDHelper are for HP ID authentication (part of the Instant Ink enrollment flow), NOT for generic user registration. The framework does not have and was never designed to have generic registration page components. This test case requires building an entirely new registration module on top of the AURA infrastructure.

recommendations: CRITICAL: Verify that this test case is intended for the AURA framework. The AURA framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment automation, NOT generic user registration. If this test is for a different application, a different framework or new module should be used.
Create a new 'registration' module within the AURA framework with: pages/registration_page.py, pages/registration_success_page.py, helper/registration_helper.py
All new page objects must inherit from BasePageObject to maintain framework consistency
Reuse test_flows_common.generate_tenant_email() for unique email generation during registration
Implement dynamic email generation with timestamp to ensure uniqueness for each test run
Add API-based test data cleanup to ensure preconditions are met (user does not already exist)
Use explicit waits with proper conditions for all element interactions and page transitions
Implement flexible navigation verification that handles both dashboard and login page outcomes
Add screenshot capture on assertion failures for debugging
Make all URLs configurable through environment configuration to support multi-environment testing
Consider creating a generic FormPage base class if multiple registration/form pages will be added in the future
Document the new registration module clearly to distinguish it from the HP Instant Ink enrollment flow
test_cases.notes: This test case represents a significant framework extension. The AURA framework currently has NO components for generic user registration. All components must be created from scratch, following the framework's patterns (BasePageObject inheritance, static helper methods, Playwright locators). The only reusable components are the 7 foundation infrastructure files. This is a PARTIALLY_RESOLVED status because the infrastructure exists but all domain-specific components are missing.

critical_gaps: No RegistrationPage page object exists in the AURA framework
No RegistrationSuccessPage page object exists in the AURA framework
No generic DashboardPage exists (only HP Instant Ink OverviewPage)
No generic LoginPage exists (only HP ID SignInPage)
No RegistrationHelper exists in the AURA framework
All 11 registration form locators must be created
All 11 registration action methods must be created
The AURA framework is designed for HP Instant Ink enrollment, NOT generic user registration
recommendations: Verify that this test case is intended for the AURA framework - there is a fundamental mismatch between the test requirements (generic user registration) and the framework's purpose (HP Instant Ink enrollment)
If this test is for the correct application, create a new 'registration' module within AURA with all required page objects and helpers
All new components must follow AURA framework patterns: inherit from BasePageObject, use static helper methods, use Playwright locators
Reuse the 7 foundation infrastructure files (test_flows_common, playwright_manager, settings, allure_reporter, base_page_object, utils, conftest)
Consider whether the AURA framework should be extended to support generic registration or if a separate framework/module is more appropriate
